response:
Sec. 1  
[THE WRITER IS CONSIDERING TWO PUBLISHERS AND NEEDS TO COMPARE THEIR OFFERS BASED ON TOTAL SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 2  
[PUBLISHER A OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF 3000 DOLLARS PLUS 5 PERCENT OF THE BOOK'S SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 3  
[PUBLISHER B OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF 1500 DOLLARS PLUS 10 PERCENT OF THE BOOK'S SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 4  
[TO DETERMINE WHEN PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE, WE SET UP AN INEQUALITY WHERE 3000 PLUS 0.05X IS GREATER THAN 1500 PLUS 0.10X.]  

Sec. 5  
[THE INEQUALITY IS 3000 + 0.05X > 1500 + 0.10X.]  

Sec. 6  
[SOLVING THE INEQUALITY, WE GET X < 30000, MEANING PUBLISHER A IS MORE BENEFICIAL WHEN SALES REVENUE IS LESS THAN 30000 DOLLARS.]  

Sec. 7  
[WITHIN THE ANTICIPATED RANGE OF 10000 TO 40000 DOLLARS, PUBLISHER A IS MORE BENEFICIAL FOR SALES REVENUE BETWEEN 10000 AND 30000 DOLLARS.]  

Sec. 8  
[THUS, THE WRITER SHOULD CHOOSE PUBLISHER A IF SHE EXPECTS SALES REVENUE TO BE IN THE RANGE OF 10000 TO 30000 DOLLARS.]
